report_name: Fraudulent 'CallPhantom' Apps on Google Play Scammed 7.3 Million Users

threat_actors.name: CallPhantom Operators
threat_actors.description: The unknown threat actors behind the CallPhantom fleeceware campaign on the Google Play store, responsible for creating and publishing 28 fraudulent applications designed to trick users into paying for non-existent services.
threat_actors.goals: Financial fraud by tricking users into paying for subscription-based services that provide only fake, randomly generated data.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The fraudulent CallPhantom apps were distributed through the official Google Play Store, abusing the platform's trust to reach millions of users.

attack_patterns.name: Masquerading
attack_patterns.description: The app creators used the deceptive developer name 'Indian gov.in' to masquerade as an official government application and gain user trust.

attack_patterns.name: Financial Theft
attack_patterns.description: The campaign's primary impact was financial theft, tricking users into subscribing to a worthless service, causing financial loss.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution
attack_patterns.description: Users were lured by false promises of retrieving call logs and willingly downloaded and installed the fraudulent applications from the Google Play Store.

malware.name: CallPhantom
malware.description: A family of 28 fleeceware applications distributed on the Google Play Store. The apps falsely promised to provide call history, SMS, and WhatsApp logs for any number, tricking users into paying for a subscription for a non-existent service.

campaigns.name: CallPhantom
campaigns.description: A large-scale fleeceware campaign on the Google Play Store involving 28 fraudulent apps that were downloaded over 7.3 million times, primarily targeting users in India and the Asia-Pacific region.

identities.name: Google
identities.description: Operator of the Google Play Store, which was abused by the CallPhantom campaign to distribute 28 fraudulent fleeceware applications. The incident represents a reputational challenge for the company.

identities.name: Android Users
identities.description: The target group of the CallPhantom campaign, with over 7.3 million users downloading the fraudulent applications, primarily in India and the Asia-Pacific region.

infrastructure.name: CallPhantom App Distribution Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The Google Play Store pages and infrastructure used to host and distribute the 28 fraudulent CallPhantom fleeceware applications.

indicators.name: CallPhantom Fleeceware Application Names
indicators.stix_pattern: [file:name IN ('Call history : any number deta', 'Call History of Any Number')]
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: CallPhantom Fleeceware Application Installation
id: 7c8d9e0f-1a2b-4c5d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of fraudulent CallPhantom fleeceware applications by filename. This is intended for use with Mobile Device Management (MDM) or Mobile Threat Defense (MTD) solutions.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/fake-call-history-apps-stole-payments.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-08
tags:
  - attack.t1474.003
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename:
      - 'Call history : any number deta'
      - 'Call History of Any Number'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the installation of known fraudulent CallPhantom fleeceware applications by their filenames.
